Acting General Counsel 
'l

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 rrJrr Street, Suite 8OO
Sacramento, California 95814
Re: Applicability of Section 84308 to Subcontractorsl
Contributions
Dear Mr. Hallabrin:
On behalf of our client, the Los Angeles County
Transportation Commission (ttCornmissi-onrr), we are requesting
confirmation of telephone advice recently provided by your staff.
The Commission members understand that they are governed by
Government Code Section 84308 as it applies to any non-
competitively bid contracts entered into by the Commission. An
issue has arisen recently as to whether the restriction 
from
accepting, soliciting or directing contributions and the
disgualification reguirements of Section 84308 applies to
contributions received from subcontractors who are specifically
named in a contract proposal before the Commission. In a
telephone conversation with our office, Johnathon Rothman of your
staff advised us that subcontractors, even though specifically
included in a contract proposal being acted upon by the
Commission, would not be considered a itpartytt to the proceeding
and, therefore, would not be included within the provisions of
Section 84308.
We would appreciate your written confirmation of this
advice. Please feel free to contact me should you have any
questions or need any further information.
Very truly yours,
DE WITT W. CLINTON
County Counsel
w ililt A qatrt;
LEEI"A A. KAPUR
Senior Deputy County Counsel
Public Services Division
